title: Process of Inference Using a Full Joint Distribution
description: A flowchart illustrating the step-by-step logical process of performing probabilistic inference using a full joint probability distribution.
elements: Define the full joint distribution over all variables
Identify the query variable (Q) and evidence variables (E)
Sum out the hidden variables (Y) to get joint of Q and E
Normalize the resulting distribution to sum to 1
Obtain the posterior probability distribution P(Q|E)
relationships: Sequential flow from defining the distribution to obtaining the final inference result
